name: Hot and Sour Shrimp with Watercress and Walnuts
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Chicken stock or canned broth
2 tbsp Catsup (Note: Jufran sauce can be used as a substitute)
1 tbsp Rice vinegar or white wine vinegar
2 medium Bell peppers, cut into 1-inch squares
8 Green onions, cut diagonally into 1-inch-long pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine shrimp, 4 tablespoons Sherry, and grated ginger in a large bowl. Cover and refrigerate for 30 minutes.
2. Mix the remaining 4 tablespoons Sherry, chicken stock or canned broth, soy sauce, catsup (or Jufran sauce), cornstarch, rice vinegar or white wine vinegar, sugar, sesame oil, and cayenne pepper in a small bowl.
3. Heat 6 tablespoons peanut oil in a wok or heavy large skillet over high heat. Add walnuts and stir-fry for 1 minute. Transfer walnuts to a plate using a slotted spoon.
4. Add watercress to the wok and stir-fry until just wilted, about 1 minute. Divide watercress among plates.
5. Add 2 tablespoons peanut oil, bell peppers, and garlic to the wok and stir-fry for 1 minute.
6. Add the remaining 2 tablespoons peanut oil, shrimp mixture, and onions to the wok and stir-fry for 1 minute.
7. Stir in the stock mixture and cook until the sauce is clear and thick, stirring frequently, about 2 minutes.
8. Spoon the sauce and shrimp over watercress. Sprinkle with walnuts and serve.
NOTE: Jufran sauce can be used instead of catsup. It has a more complex and interesting taste. Use cautiously when adding cayenne pepper. Makes four servings; approximately 265 calories per serving.
